items.text: Do you need extra pillows to breathe comfortably while lying down, or can you not lie flat at all?
choices: Needs one extra pillow to breathe comfortably

items.text: Have you woken up gasping for air and needed to sit up to catch your breath?
choices: Wakes gasping for air, relieved by sitting up

items.text: Have you noticed new or worsening swelling in your legs or belly?
choices: New abdominal distention or early satiety

items.text: Have you gained several pounds in less than a week?
choices: Weight gain of several pounds over less than a week

items.text: Have you missed any doses of your water pill or other heart failure medications?
choices: Missed one or more diuretic or heart failure medication doses

items.text: Have you eaten anything unusually salty recently, like takeout or processed food?
choices: Recent high-sodium meal or dietary indiscretion

items.text: Have you been told you have heart failure before, and if so, was your heart pumping normally or weakly?
choices: Known heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
Known heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

items.text: Have you had any chest pressure with exertion along with the breathing trouble?
Have you felt your heart racing or fluttering, or do you have a known history of atrial fibrillation?
choices: No palpitations or known atrial fibrillation
Known atrial fibrillation, possibly with a fast rate

items.text: Have you had a fever or a new or worsening cough?
Have you taken any ibuprofen, naproxen, or other NSAID pain relievers recently?
Have you noticed you're urinating less than usual?
Have you felt lightheaded or actually passed out along with this?
Have you been hospitalized for heart failure before, and if so, does this feel similar or worse than those times?
choices: Prior admissions, this episode feels similar
Prior admissions, this episode is more severe than usual

items.text: Assess jugular venous pressure.
choices: Elevated jugular venous pressure with prominent v waves

items.text: Assess hepatojugular reflux.
Auscultate the lungs for crackles.
Assess work of breathing.
Auscultate for an S3 gallop.
Auscultate for a new or changed murmur.
Assess heart rate and rhythm regularity.
Inspect and palpate the lower extremities for edema.
choices: Mild pitting edema to the ankles
Marked pitting edema extending above the knees

items.text: Assess the abdomen for ascites or hepatomegaly.
Assess extremity warmth and capillary refill for perfusion status.
choices: Cool extremities with delayed capillary refill
Cool, mottled extremities with thready pulses

items.text: Measure and compare weight to the documented dry weight.
choices: At or near documented dry weight

items.text: Assess blood pressure for hypertensive or hypotensive decompensation.
